title: Stroke Ischemic Core / Penumbra Classification from CT-Perfusion (PWDR)
sub_domain: Cerebral hemodynamics recovery from dynamic CT-perfusion with mismatch-based stroke triage readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-029 CT (existing in v1 genesis) with deterministic CT-perfusion threshold rules (CBV / CBF / Tmax) per RAPID / Olea Sphere / Siemens Syngo.via clinical-validation cohorts to assign stroke triage labels {no_lesion, ischemic_core, penumbra_at_risk, large_vessel_occlusion_candidate}. Universal in acute stroke imaging (DAWN, DEFUSE-3 trial criteria) — every Level-1 stroke center performs CTP-based mechanical thrombectomy triage. UTSW Stroke Center Level-1 designation.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on stroke-triage categorical readout {no_lesion, ischemic_core, penumbra, mismatch_eligible_LVO} per voxel and per region

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Per-voxel: ischemic_core = (rCBF < 0.30 of contralateral) OR (CBV < 2 mL/100g); penumbra = (Tmax > 6 s) AND NOT ischemic_core. Per-patient triage: mismatch_eligible_LVO = (penumbra_volume - core_volume > 15 mL) AND (penumbra/core ratio > 1.8) per DAWN/DEFUSE-3 criteria. Time window: thrombectomy candidate = onset 0-24 h with mismatch criteria. ASPECTS score: 10-point regional grading from non-contrast CT.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: voxel-resolved cerebral hemodynamic parameters (CBF [mL/100g/min], CBV [mL/100g], MTT [s], Tmax [s]) recovered via deconvolution from time-series CT-perfusion data using L1-029 CT as the projection-reconstruction core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: CTP thresholds are step functions on continuous hemodynamic parameter values (rCBF, CBV, Tmax); per-class boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes. Volume aggregation is Lipschitz (sum of indicator-thresholded continuous quantities). Smoothed-classification globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.06 per percentage-point of perfusion-parameter error.

related_principles: L1-029 (CT — projection / reconstruction analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-039 (X-ray angio — sibling vascular-imaging modality, informational)

E.description: Stroke Ischemic Core / Penumbra Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-029 CT analytical core extended for dynamic perfusion imaging with established CTP threshold rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-029 + dynamic deconvolution): from time-series CTP acquisition (typically 60-90 frames over 50-70 seconds following IV iodinated contrast bolus), recover voxel-resolved hemodynamic parameter maps via SVD-based deconvolution against arterial input function: CBF (cerebral blood flow), CBV (cerebral blood volume), MTT (mean transit time), Tmax (time to maximum residue function). Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply RAPID / Olea / Syngo.via thresholds to identify ischemic core (rCBF<0.30 or CBV<2) and penumbra (Tmax>6s); compute volumes; classify into mechanical thrombectomy triage. Difficulty tier delta = 5. Mismatch parameters: AIF_selection_uncertainty, motion_during_acquisition, partial_volume_at_arteries, contrast_bolus_timing, deconvolution_regularization, threshold_calibration_vendor_difference.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-029 + temporal deconvolution): {y_proj(t)} -> mu_attenuation(r, t); deconvolution(C(r, t), AIF(t)) -> R(r, t) residue function -> CBF, CBV, MTT, Tmax. Stage 2: ischemic_core_mask = (rCBF<0.30) | (CBV<2); penumbra_mask = (Tmax>6) & ~core_mask; triage = mismatch-volume rules per DAWN/DEFUSE-3.
E.world_state_x: Per-patient triage label + per-voxel categorical map (core / penumbra / no-lesion) + ischemic core volume [mL] + penumbra volume [mL] + mismatch ratio.
E.observation_y: Dynamic CT-perfusion volume series (typically 512x512 in-plane, 8-32 z-slices, 60-90 time frames, 1-3 second temporal resolution).

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.dynamic_reconstruction -> L.deconvolution_aif -> L.cbf_cbv_mtt_tmax -> L.ctp_threshold_classifier -> int.spatial_temporal
coupling_constraints: temporal_dynamics: time-series CT projections couple to per-voxel attenuation evolution C(r, t); deconvolution against AIF(t) recovers residue function R(r, t)
perfusion_parameters: CBF, CBV, MTT, Tmax are jointly derived from R(r, t) via central volume principle CBV = CBF * MTT and Tmax = argmax R(r, t)

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-029. Uniqueness conditional on AIF selection (manual or automated) and temporal sampling adequacy. Stability conditional with deconvolution_regularization dominant for noise sensitivity; threshold_calibration_vendor_difference contributes inter-vendor bias (~20% volume estimation difference between RAPID and Olea). Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled dynamic-CTP + perfusion-threshold forward established by Ostergaard 1996 (foundational SVD deconvolution), Konstas 2009 (CTP technical review), Wintermark 2006 (perfusion thresholds), Albers 2018 (DEFUSE-3 trial), Nogueira 2018 (DAWN trial), Goyal 2016 (HERMES meta-analysis).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [L1-029 reconstruction + SVD/oSVD deconvolution + threshold] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepStroke, RAPID Vision, Brainomix e-Stroke] with explicit physics-informed perfusion-parameter regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-029 reconstruction bound + deconvolution noise propagation: ||{CBF, CBV, MTT, Tmax}_hat - true||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise_per_voxel * sqrt(N_time_frames). Volume estimation bound: |V_core_hat - V_core| <= C2 * |threshold_uncertainty| * voxel_volume. Misclassification rate ~5-10% in clinical validation.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * N_time_frames * log(N_time_frames)) per iteration; deep neural variants O(H W Z N_time_frames * F_theta_cost)

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify stroke patients into thrombectomy candidates from CTP'. Option-3 recast: 'Recover voxel-resolved CBF/CBV/MTT/Tmax via dynamic CT + deconvolution; apply RAPID / Olea / Syngo.via thresholds for core / penumbra; compute mismatch volumes per DAWN/DEFUSE-3'. Feasible because cerebral perfusion has well-defined indicator-dilution forward model and triage rules are deterministic from recovered hemodynamic parameters.

joint_well_posedness_references: Ostergaard L et al. (1996) High resolution measurement of cerebral blood flow using intravascular tracer bolus passages. Part I: Mathematical approach and statistical analysis. Magn Reson Med 36(5):715-725.
Konstas AA et al. (2009) Theoretic basis and technical implementations of CT perfusion in acute ischemic stroke. AJNR Am J Neuroradiol 30(4):662-668.

Wintermark M et al. (2006) Perfusion-CT assessment of infarct core and penumbra: receiver operating characteristic curve analysis in 130 patients suspected of acute hemispheric stroke. Stroke 37(4):979-985.
Albers GW et al. (2018) Thrombectomy for stroke at 6 to 16 hours with selection by perfusion imaging (DEFUSE 3). NEJM 378(8):708-718.
Nogueira RG et al. (2018) Thrombectomy 6 to 24 hours after stroke with a mismatch between deficit and infarct (DAWN). NEJM 378(1):11-21.
Goyal M et al. (2016) Endovascular thrombectomy after large-vessel ischaemic stroke: a meta-analysis of individual patient data from five randomised trials (HERMES). Lancet 387(10029):1723-1731.
Bivard A et al. (2014) Defining the extent of irreversible brain ischemia using perfusion computed tomography. Cerebrovasc Dis 37(2):87-94.

distinctness_audit.advisory_label: Distinct (parent-child PWDR pattern with temporal extension)
distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-029 CT recovers static 3D attenuation; L1-524 extends to 4D dynamic CTP with deconvolution and adds stroke-triage threshold rules. Different solution_space (1D triage label + per-voxel core/penumbra map vs 3D static attenuation); adds L.dynamic_reconstruction, L.deconvolution_aif, L.cbf_cbv_mtt_tmax, L.ctp_threshold_classifier primitives plus n_c = 2 (vs L1-029's n_c = 0).

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in acute stroke care at every Level-1 stroke center globally. Mechanical thrombectomy is the standard of care for large-vessel occlusion (LVO) ischemic stroke; CTP-based triage extends the treatment window to 6-24 hours per DAWN/DEFUSE-3 trial criteria. UTSW Stroke Center holds Level-1 designation. Commercial systems: RAPID (iSchemaView, FDA-cleared), Olea Sphere, Siemens Syngo.via, GE CT Perfusion 4D, Brainomix e-Stroke — all are PWDR-shaped systems. PWDR formulation gives physics-grounded explainability: triage decisions derive from explicit perfusion-parameter thresholds tied to clinical-trial validation cohorts.
